channel.title: The Open Stacks - recently added
channel.description: Newest additions to The Open Stacks, an anti-censorship library.

item.title: O\u2019Neill
item.author: Arthur and Barbara Gelb
item.description: Acknowledgments A great many persons, libraries, institutions, organizations and publications have given their help and cooperation in the preparation of this book. We will never be able to thank all of them adequately,

item.title: Bloody Rule and a Cannibal Order!
item.author: Ash P. Morgans
item.description: Part I: The Egoist Many problems rear their heads when attempting to establish moral systems. Determining proper criteria, defining rights and wrongs, or establishing a certain degree of objectivity are just some of the

item.title: Saint Tantalus
item.author: Benjamin De Casseres
item.description: Saint Tantalus To THE DIOSCURI JOHN COWPER POWYS and LLEWELYN POWYS \u201cAnd so these two sons of Zeus ascended to the heavens together and became the Constellation Gemini\u2014known to men as Castor and Pollux.\u201d FOREWORD \u201cSaint

item.title: Liberty Vol. IV. No. 19.
item.author: Benjamin Tucker
item.description: \u201cFor always in thine eyes, O Liberty! Shines that high light whereby the world is saved; And though thou slay us, we will trust in thee.\u201d John Hay. Anarchists\u2019 Aims Stated in Rhyme. Written for and published in the Melbo

item.title: Liberty Vol. IV. No. 24.
item.author: Benjamin Tucker
item.description: \u201cFor always in thine eyes, O Liberty! Shines that high light whereby the world is saved; And though thou slay us, we will trust in thee.\u201d John Hay. On Picket Duty. The Avelings have gone to Australia to preach Socialism.

item.title: Liberty Vol. IV. No. 25.
item.author: Benjamin Tucker
item.description: \u201cFor always in thine eyes, O Liberty! Shines that high light whereby the world is saved; And though thou slay us, we will trust in thee.\u201d John Hay. On Picket Duty. The immigration problem has received some attention from

item.title: Liberty Vol. V. No. 1.
item.author: Benjamin Tucker
item.description: \u201cFor always in thine eyes, O Liberty! Shines that high light whereby the world is saved; And though thou slay us, we will trust in thee.\u201d John Hay. The Last Man. [Read at the Subscription Dinner connected with the Scrant

item.title: Chomsky on the Nod
item.author: Bob Black
item.description: Chomsky on Anarchism. By Noam Chomsky. Selected and edited by Barry Pateman. Edinburgh, Scotland and Oakland, California: AK Press, 2005. Occupy. By Noam Chomsky. Brooklyn, New York: Zuccotti Park Press, 2012. Let me jus

item.title: The Abolition of Work and Other Essays
item.author: Bob Black
item.description: may be freely reproduced, translated or adapted, even without mentioning the source. notes Cover by Donna Kossy isbn 0-915179-41-5 Dedication: To Donna Kossy Foreward by Ed Lawrence | BOB BLACK: TEXT AND CONTEXT by Ed La

item.title: Withered Anarchism
item.author: Bob Black
item.description: \u201cThe general level of insight now is more educated, curiosity is wide awake, and judgments are made more quickly than formerly; so the feet of them which shall carry thee out are already at the door\u201d \u2014 Hegel[1] The tale

item.title: And Yet It Moves
item.author: Boy Igor
item.description: Introduction: The Death of Science Of all the spectacles science is the least attacked. In a society where power is everywhere diffused and everywhere attacked, the appeal to science has become a final authority. All tha

item.title: And Yet It Moves (Updated Edition)
item.author: Boy Igor & Phil Mailer
item.description: Preface c/o Endangered Phoenix This small booklet was written in the 1980s just before the fall of the Berlin Wall and the subsequent collapse of the Soviet Union and its Stalinist influence throughout Eastern Europe. It

item.title: Pagan Anarchism
item.author: Christopher Scott Thompson
item.description: Synopsis | ~~ Our newest offering by poet, anarchist, and Pagan Christopher Scott Thompson, Pagan Anarchism explores the history of anti-authoritarian and anti-capitalist movements and discovers alongside them a fierce P

item.title: Human Rights After Deleuze
item.author: Christos Marneros
item.description: Abstract | ~~ This book examines the possibility of creating new ways of existing beyond human rights. Multiple socio-political crises and the dominance of neoliberal and capitalist policies have led legal and political

item.title: Fetishism as social creativity
item.author: David Graeber
item.description: ABSTRACT Originally, the term \u2018fetishes\u2019 was used by European merchants to refer to objects employed in West Africa to make and enforce agreements, often between people with almost nothing in common. They thus provide an

item.title: The Cynics and Stoics
item.author: Diogenes La\u00ebrtius
item.description: The Cynics Antisthenes 1. Antisthenes,[1] the son of Antisthenes, was an Athenian. It was said, however, that he was not of pure Attic blood. Hence his reply to one who taunted him with this: \u201cThe mother of the gods too

item.title: Loaves and Fishes
item.author: Dorothy Day
item.description: Synopsis | ~~ Marking the centenary of Dorothy Day\u2019s birth in 1897, this new edition of Loaves and Fishes makes a modern religious classic available to a new generation. A companion to her autobiography, The Long Lonelin

item.title: Ethics and Revolution
item.author: Edward Abramowski
item.description: I It is impossible to find a more characteristic feature of socialist politics than the method by which it tries to carry out its reforms. This method is based on the fact that the reforms are not the product of the ruli

item.title: Encyclopedie des Nuisances
item.description: Online archive of English translations of Encyclopedia of Nuisances , a post-situationist review published from 1984-1992.

item.title: Enragés and Situationists in the occupation movement, France, May ’68
item.description: The contemporaneous and lively Situationist account of the near-revolutionary events of May 1968 in Paris, written in the fray by one of the major participants.

item.title: Why Anarchists Like Zen?
item.author: Enrique Galva\u0301n-A\u0301lvarez
item.description: Most attempts to formulate a Buddhist anarchism in the West take Zen Buddhism as their reference point, often disregarding other Buddhist traditions and their anarchic/libertarian potential. In response to these early We

item.title: Occult Features of Anarchism
item.author: Erica Lagalisse
item.description: Abstract By exploring the hidden correspondences between classical anarchism, Renaissance magic and occult philosophy, this chapter advances the critical study of Left-political attachment to the \u2018secular\u2019, wherein Weste

item.title: Ethiopia and Socialist Theory: The Blood on the Wall
item.description: Ken Tarbuck was a member of several different Trotskyist groups in the until the early 1970s. He went to Ethiopia to work as a university lecturer in 1977 and published this analysis in 1992.

item.title: The Right To Be Greedy
item.author: For Ourselves
item.description: The positive conception of egoism, the perspective of communist egoism, is the very heart and unity of our theoretical and practical coherence. This perspective is the essence of what separates us from both the left and

item.title: Illyria Street Commune
item.author: Fredy Perlman
item.description: to Donna \u2014 Ben A note on performing Illyria Street Commune A dash at the end of a speech indicates that the next speaker begins before the previous one is finished. In general, there should be no pauses; props can be car

item.title: The Incoherence of the Intellectual
item.author: Fredy Perlman
item.description: INTRODUCTION Man as History\u2011Maker The philosophers have only interpreted the world in various ways; the point, however, is to change it. \u2014 Karl Marx For C. Wright Mills, the most important issue of political reflection\u2014a

item.title: The Gay Science
item.author: Friedrich Nietzsche
item.description: EDITORIAL NOTE "The Joyful Wisdom," written in 1882, just before "Zarathustra," is rightly judged to be one of Nietzsche's best books. Here the essentially grave and masculine face of the poet-philosopher is seen to ligh

item.title: The Genealogy of Morals
item.author: Friedrich Nietzsche
item.description: THE GENEALOGY OF MORALS A POLEMIC BY FRIEDRICH NIETZSCHE CONTENTS. [PREFACE.](PREFACE) [FIRST ESSAY. \u201cGOOD AND EVIL,\u201d \u201cGOOD AND BAD.\u201d](FIRST-ESSAY) [SECOND ESSAY. \u201cGUILT,\u201d \u201cBAD CONSCIENCE,\u201d AND THE LIKE.](SECOND-ESSAY) [

item.title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra
item.author: Friedrich Nietzsche
item.description: First Part. Zarathustra\u2019s Discourses. Zarathustra\u2019s Prologue. 1. When Zarathustra was thirty years old, he left his home and the lake of his home, and went into the mountains. There he enjoyed his spirit and solitude, an

item.title: Twilight of the Idols
item.author: Friedrich Nietzsche
item.description: Preface Maintaining cheerfulness in the midst of a gloomy task, fraught with immeasurable responsibility, is no small feat; and yet what is needed more than cheerfulness? Nothing succeeds if prankishness has no part in i

item.title: The Law of Freedom in a Platform
item.author: Gerrard Winstanley
item.description: CHAP. I. - The Law of Freedom. The great searching of heart in these days is to find out where true freedom lies, that the commonwealth of England might be established in peace. Some say, 'It lies in the free use of trad

item.title: The Continuing Appeal of Religion
item.author: Gilles Dauv\u00e9 & Karl Nesic
item.description: Introduction This is a modified version of an essay published in French as the 7th Lettre de Troploin, June 2006, called Le Pr\u00e9sent d\u2019une illusion. The English title is inspired by Fredy Perlman\u2019s The Continuing Appeal o

item.title: The return of ethnographic theory
item.author: Giovanni Da Col and David Graeber
item.description: Take an ethnographer. She has spent more than thirty months in the Bocage in Mayenne studying witchcraft. \u2018How exciting, how thrilling, how extraordinary...! Tell us all about the witches\u2019, she is asked again and again w

item.title: Guy Debord obituary
item.description: An obituary of Guy Debord written immediately after his death in 1994. The article in Freedom newspaper was headlined "The author of Society of the Spectacle has killed himself".

item.title: Pirate Utopias
item.author: Hakim Bey
item.description: I. Pirate and Mermaid[1] [1] \u201cThe Legend of El-Minar.\u201d Chimenti (1965) Some years ago a tall tower stood at the extreme end of Cape Marabata; the Christians called Torre Blanquilla (White Tower) and it was known to the M

item.title: T.A.Z.: The Temporary Autonomous Zone, Ontological Anarchy, Poetic Terrorism
item.author: Hakim Bey
item.description: Acknowledgments Chaos: The Broadsheets of Ontological Anarchism was first published in 1985 by Grim Reaper Press of Weehawken, New Jersey; a later re-issue was published in Providence, Rhode Island, and this edition was

item.title: Ethical Individualism
item.author: Han Ryner
item.description: Translator\u2019s Introduction At the dawn of the 20th century, Europe hosted a brief blossoming of individualistic thought. Inspired by the great thinkers of the preceding century such as Stirner, Ibsen, Nietzsche, and Spenc

item.title: Here and Now magazine
item.description: Complete online archive of scanned issues of Here and Now, a UK-based situationist magazine published 1985-1994.

item.title: Implications #1 (1975)
item.description: First and only issue of this post-situationist journal from California, edited by Isaac Cronin and Chris Shutes.

item.title: In the cauldron of the negative - Jean-Marc Mandosio
item.description: A critique of the Situationist International, emphasizing the divergent trajectories of Guy Debord and Raoul Vaneigem, focusing on the role of the concept of alchemy in the SI’s theory of the revolution, with discussions of, among other topics, revolution as “transmutation”, the alchemical proto-dialectic and its relation to the Hegelian-Marxist dialectic of “supersession”, Vaneigem’s alleged debt

item.title: Deschooling Society
item.author: Ivan Illich
item.description: Introduction I owe my interest in public education to Everett Reimer. Until we first met in Puerto Rico in 1958, I had never questioned the value of extending obligatory schooling to all people. Together we have come to

item.title: The Karezza Method
item.author: J. William Lloyd
item.description: 1. PREFACE It was, I believe, in the winter of 1915-16 that a woman-friend in California wrote and asked me why I did not write a special little book on Karezza. As events had convinced me that there certainly was crying

item.title: Propaganda
item.author: Jacques Ellul
item.description: date source Translated from the French by Konrad Kellen and Jean Lerner. With an introduction by Konrad Kellen. lang en pubdate 2023-10-11T03:58:26 topics philosophy, sociology cover j-e-jacques-ellul-propaganda-1.jpg In

item.title: The Philosophy of Egoism
item.author: James L. Walker
item.description: I We seek understanding of facts for guidance in action, for avoidance of mistake and suffering, and even for resignation to the inevitable. This statement may cover the chief aims of mankind in intellectual discussion,

item.title: Methodological Anarchism
item.author: Jason Lee Byas and Billy Christmas
item.description: I. Introduction Anarchists all share the same basic public policy proposal: abolish public policy. With regard to foreign policy, their position is to abolish the military. With regard to education policy, abolish state

item.title: Clarifying the Unique and Its Self-Creation
item.author: Jason McQuinn
item.description: \u201cThe World has languished long enough under the tyranny of thought, under the terrorism of ideas; she is waking from the heavy dream....\u201d \u2014 Max Stirner, \u201cThe Philosophical Reactionaries\u201d (1847) Max Stirner\u2019s 1844 masterw

item.title: Repudiating the Stalinist Legacy
item.author: Javier Sethness-Castro
item.description: Totalitarian Propaganda that Fails in Rationalizing his World-Historical Crimes \u201cToday [in 1958], everyone knows Russian Communism as the greatest barbarism on earth. Stalin is the name which symbolizes this.\u201d \u2013 Raya Dun

item.title: Max Stirner\u2019s Dialectical Egoism
item.author: John F Welsh
item.description: ; Published by Lexington Books ; A division of Rowman & Littlefield Publishers, Inc. ; A wholly owned subsidiary of The Rowman & Littlefield Publishing Group, Inc. ; 4501 Forbes Boulevard, Suite 200, Lanham, Maryland 207

item.title: Ayn Rand\u2019s Second-Hand Ethics
item.author: JudgeSabo
item.description: Ayn Rand is a fascinating figure in the history of right-wing laissez-faire politics. She was primarily known, and is still remembered today, as the author of the novels The Fountainhead and Atlas Shrugged, acting as a \u201c

item.title: Anarchism and Education
item.author: Judith Suissa
item.description: Preface It is nearly five years since the first publication of this book. Reflecting on the work that went into it, and on the discussions that it has prompted with friends and colleagues over the years, there are two po
